title: Multiplex fluorescence in situ hybridization using RNAScope on paraffin embedded human nervous system tissue with optional photobleaching for autofluorescence mitigation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f6dag","text":"This protocol is to perform the RNAScope in situ hybridization procedure in human central nervous system tissue with modifications from our newly established method to reduce endogenous autofluorescence from lipofuscin. This procedure should increase signal to noise by drastically reducing fluorescence background during microscopic image capture.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Bake paraffin-embedded slides in oven at 60 °C for 01:00:00

During baking, prepare staining bins (for dipping microscopy slides). 2 staining bins will be needed filled with fresh xylenes, and 2 with fresh 95% ethanol. Xylenes should be handled in a chemical fume hood.
Place slides in plastic slide holder and perform the following rinses in a chemical fume hood. Agitate briefly: Wash in xylenes (x2) 00:05:00 Wash in 95% ethanol (x2) 00:02:00

During the xylene wash steps above, set a hot plate to 100 °C . Heat 200 mL of 1x target retrieval solution. Cover with foil to insulate, and maintain the temperature at 100 °C ± 2 °C by monitoring with a temperature probe. Heat at this temperature for 00:15:00 .
Set cold plate to 2°C.
Let ethanol evaporate off of the slides (dry completely).
Add H 2 0 2 in drops to cover tissue sections, and let sit for 00:10:00 at Room temperature .

During H 2 0 2 application step, begin preparing probes. a. Warm probes at 40 °C for 00:10:00 , then cool to Room temperature . b. Spin Channel 2-4 probes (C2-C4) down to collect liquid at the bottom of the tube. c. Add necessary volumes of probes to one tube - total volume varies as needed, with approximately 50:1:1:1 ratio of Channel 1 (C1) probe to C2-C4. (ex. add 1 µL of C2, C3 and C4 probes to 47 µL C1 probe.) d. Label probe mixes and gently agitate to mix.
Prepare 2 staining bins of distilled H 2 0 (dH 2 0).
Tap off H 2 0 2 (stored at 4 °C ) from slides, and rinse briefly in dH 2 0 (x2).
Place sections in pre-prepared 100 °C 1x target retrieval buffer (step 3.1) for 00:25:00 . Target retrieval step can vary by tissue type. The general guideline is 15-25 minutes.
Prepare staining bin of room temperature dH 2 0, and staining bin of 95% ethanol.
Rinse sections in dH 2 0 for 00:00:15 , then rinse in 95% ethanol for 00:03:00 . Let slides dry completely after rinsing in ethanol.
Draw on hydrophobic barrier around sections. Allow to dry for 00:05:00 . We recommend Vector ImmEdge® Pen (Cat no. H-4000). The choice of pen in this step is critical for desired results.

Add protease plus reagent to slides dropwise to cover samples. Place in oven to bake at 40 °C for 00:30:00 . Protease reagent type, and time of this digest can vary by tissue type. If subsequent immunohistochemistry is desired, a less stringent protein digest may be beneficial.
Prepare slide tray for oven with blotting/filter paper on the bottom (soaked in dH 2 0) to keep slides from drying out during the subsequent heating steps.
Prepare a staining bin of dH 2 0.
Tap off protease reagent, and wash slides in dH 2 0 briefly with agitation.
Tap off dH 2 0 and cover samples with probe mixture. Return slides to oven at 40 °C for 02:00:00 to hybridize probes to tissue.
Prepare 2 staining bins of 1x wash buffer. To prepare the wash buffer, warm the 50x stock to 40 °C and dilute in dH 2 0.
Wash slides in 1x wash buffer twice for 00:02:00 with initial agitation.
Set out kit reagents for Amp1-3 and HRP-C1, -C2, -C3, -C4 and HRP blocker and allow to come to room temperature.

The following steps are repetitive wash and incubation steps. This checklist is useful to track progress over time. Checklist for day 2 protocol procedures. The following steps are repetitive wash and incubation steps. This checklist is useful to track progress over time. * Row 5 is an additional set of steps performed for four-plex only. Individuals performing three-plex can stop after completing Row 4.

Place slides inside a clean clear plastic bag to prevent condensation from forming on top of the slides. Use plastic spacers (such as caps of 50 ml tubes) to prevent contact between bag and slides. For formalin fixed paraffin embedded (FFPE) slides, do not deparaffinize at this stage. For optimal results for visualization of mRNAs, cut sections should be stored at -80°C until use.
Incubate slides for 24-72 hours under light exposure.
Fill staining bin with 5x SSC buffer (from 50x stock) and store samples overnight for day 2.
Wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer.
Prepare slide tray for oven with blotting/filter paper on bottom soaked with dH 2 0 to keep slides from dying out while in oven.
Line 1 of above chart: Tap off liquid and add Amp1 to cover tissue within hydrophobic barrier. Incubate at 40 °C for 00:30:00 . Tap off Amp1 and wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer (twice).
Tap off liquid and add Amp2 to cover tissue within hydrophobic barrier. Incubate at 40 °C for 00:30:00 . Tap off Amp2 and wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer (twice).
Tap off liquid and add Amp3 to cover tissue within hydrophobic barrier. Incubate at 40 °C for 00:15:00 . Tap off Amp3 and wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer (twice).
Line 2 of above chart: Tap off liquid and add HRP-C1 to cover tissue within hydrophobic barrier. Incubate at 40 °C for 00:15:00 . Tap off HRP-C1 and wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer (twice).
Line 3 of above chart: Tap off liquid and add HRP-C2 to cover tissue within hydrophobic barrier. Incubate at 40 °C for 00:15:00 . Tap off HRP-C2 and wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer (twice).
Line 4 of above chart: Tap off liquid and add HRP-C3 to cover tissue within hydrophobic barrier. Incubate at 40 °C for 00:15:00 . Tap off HRP-C3 and wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer (twice).
Line 5 of above chart: Tap off liquid and add HRP-C4 to cover tissue within hydrophobic barrier. Incubate at 40 °C for 00:15:00 . Tap off HRP-C4 and wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer (twice).
Tap off liquid and add 150-200 µl diluted fluorophore for C1 to each slide. Incubate at 40 °C for 00:30:00 . Tap off fluorophore and wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer (twice).
Tap off liquid and add 150-200 µl diluted fluorophore for C2 to each slide. Incubate at 40 °C for 00:30:00 . Tap off fluorophore and wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer (twice).
Tap off liquid and add 150-200 µl diluted fluorophore for C3 to each slide. Incubate at 40 °C for 00:30:00 . Tap off fluorophore and wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer (twice).
Tap off liquid and add 150-200 µl diluted fluorophore for C4 to each slide. Incubate at 40 °C for 00:30:00 . Tap off fluorophore and wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer (twice).
Tap off liquid and add HRP blocker to cover tissue. Incubate at 40 °C for 00:15:00 . Tap off HRP blocker and wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer (twice).
Tap off liquid and add HRP blocker to cover tissue. Incubate at 40 °C for 00:15:00 . Tap off HRP blocker and wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer (twice).
Tap off liquid and add HRP blocker to cover tissue. Incubate at 40 °C for 00:15:00 . Tap off HRP blocker and wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer (twice).
Tap off liquid and add HRP blocker to cover tissue. Incubate at 40 °C for 00:15:00 . Tap off HRP blocker and wash slides for ~2 min in 1x wash buffer (twice).
Add ~4 drops of DAPI solution to each slide and incubate for ~30 seconds at room temperature, then immediately tap off.

Coverslip using Immu-Mount and store 30 min to overnight in the dark. Longer term storage should be done at 4°C. Immu-Mount is useful because it is reversible if multiple rounds of staining are desired.
creator: Matthew R. Sapio
